_meta.purpose: Extension A hand-curated Open Dental mapping. Consumed by praxis_deid/extractors/a_*.py to produce treatment_plans_raw.csv at de-id time. Derived from expected_mapping_open_dental.json + expert audit. DO NOT regenerate from the wizard — the wizard's output is 78% reliable per the Wizard-1 validation gate (commit 6b19ae9). Hand-edits to this file MUST cite the Open Dental manual page they are based on.

source_id.notes: Direct PK on the treatment plan table.

provider_id.notes: Open Dental's treatplan table has no direct ProvNum; falls back to the planning provider on the constituent procedures (proctp.ProvNum). When a plan has procedures by multiple providers, this picks one — operator should confirm selection rule.

presented_date.notes: DateTP is the canonical 'date plan was presented' field.

accepted_date.notes: Open Dental records patient signature time as DateTSigned. Practices that don't use electronic signatures will have NULLs here even for accepted plans — flag for operator review.

declined_date.notes: Open Dental has no explicit 'declined' date column. Practices typically infer decline from 'plan not signed within X days'. Recommend post-extraction derivation rather than mapping.

expired_date.notes: Open Dental does not auto-expire treatment plans; no source column exists.

status.source_expression: CASE WHEN treatplan.DateTSigned IS NOT NULL THEN 'accepted' WHEN treatplan.TPStatus = 1 THEN 'declined' ELSE 'presented' END
status.notes: TPStatus enum: 0=Active, 1=Inactive (treated here as 'declined' because Open Dental practices most commonly mark plans Inactive after a documented patient refusal — but it can also mean expired or superseded; operator MUST confirm). DateTSigned presence is the most reliable accepted-signal.

plan_dollars.source_expression: (SELECT SUM(proctp.FeeAmt) FROM proctp WHERE proctp.TreatPlanNum = treatplan.TreatPlanNum)
plan_dollars.notes: Sum of constituent procedure fees on the plan. Excludes tax (typically 0 in dental).

procedure_category.notes: Open Dental stores ADA D-codes on proctp.ProcCode but no canonical category. The de-id tool's existing procedure_category mapping (per the v0.1 contract) would need to be applied at a higher level. Mark NULL for now and let downstream classify.

transformations.status: CASE WHEN treatplan.DateTSigned IS NOT NULL THEN 'accepted' WHEN treatplan.TPStatus = 1 THEN 'expired' ELSE 'presented' END

notes: Open Dental treatment plans don't expose a clean lifecycle — accepted/declined/expired must be derived from a combination of DateTSigned, TPStatus, and time-since-presentation. Recommend a follow-up review with the practice on their workflow conventions before relying on the status field.
GROUND TRUTH AUDIT: TPStatus=1 mapped to 'declined' rather than 'expired' (per Open Dental docs, TPStatus=1 is Inactive — the practice manually deactivated; usually post-refusal).
